dumanIsiTahliye.subtitle: Controlled evacuation of smoke and heat during fires; regulation-compliant, engineering-supported, end-to-end solutions.
dumanIsiTahliye.ctaPrimary: Get a Quote for Your Project
dumanIsiTahliye.whyImportantTitle: The First Threat in a Fire is Smoke
dumanIsiTahliye.whyImportantP1: Fire statistics show that most casualties are caused not by flames, but by dense smoke and toxic gases. An effective smoke extraction system keeps escape routes visible and breathable; facilitates firefighter intervention; minimizes structural damage.
dumanIsiTahliye.whyImportantP2: NOVVES manages the entire process from design to commissioning with its own engineering team. CFD analysis is performed for each project to simulate fan placement, smoke spread scenarios, and evacuation times.

steps.label: Production & Installation

steps.label: Commissioning & Maintenance
steps.desc: Testing, reporting, periodic inspection and 24/7 service support

areas.title: Enclosed Car Parks
areas.description: CO extraction + smoke evacuation during fires

faqItems.q: In which buildings is a smoke extraction system mandatory?
faqItems.a: Smoke extraction systems are mandatory in enclosed car parks, shopping centers, high-rise buildings, tunnels, and industrial facilities according to fire regulations. Contact us for a project-specific needs analysis.

faqItems.q: What does EN 12101-3 certification mean?
faqItems.a: EN 12101-3 is the European standard that certifies the capacity of smoke and heat extraction fans to operate for a specified period under fire conditions (high temperature). NOVVES products are manufactured in accordance with this standard.

faqItems.q: Why is CFD analysis necessary?
faqItems.a: CFD analysis enables the simulation of jet fan placement, smoke spread scenarios, and evacuation times in a computer environment. This verifies system performance before installation and documents regulation compliance.

faqItems.q: Can integration with existing systems be done?
faqItems.a: Yes, NOVVES smoke extraction systems can be integrated with existing BMS infrastructure, fire detection panels, and automation systems.

dumanIsiTahliye.finalCtaTitle: Do you need a smoke extraction system?
dumanIsiTahliye.finalCtaDesc: Contact our expert engineering team for survey, CFD analysis, engineering and quotation processes.

faqItems.q: What is the difference between an AHU and a split air conditioner?
faqItems.a: An AHU is a central system that provides homogeneous temperature, humidity control and filtration across large areas. Split units operate on an individual room basis. For commercial buildings, central AHU systems are far more efficient.

faqItems.q: How much energy does heat recovery save?
faqItems.a: Depending on operating conditions, 50-70% energy savings can be achieved. Heat energy from exhaust air is transferred to fresh air, significantly reducing heating/cooling loads.

faqItems.q: Can an HVAC system be installed in existing buildings?
faqItems.a: Yes, NOVVES modular AHUs can be adapted to existing buildings with their compact dimensions. The most suitable solution is proposed after a site survey.

faqItems.q: What are the maintenance intervals?
faqItems.a: Filter replacement is typically done every 3-6 months. General system maintenance is recommended at least twice a year. NOVVES periodic maintenance contracts ensure your systems run efficiently at all times.

hijyenikFiltrasyon.whyImportantP1: In hospital operating rooms, pharmaceutical production facilities, food processing areas and laboratories, airborne particulate and microbiological contamination directly affects human health and product quality.

faqItems.q: How often should HEPA filters be replaced?
faqItems.a: HEPA filter life varies between 2-5 years depending on operating conditions. Regular replacement of pre-filters and bag filters significantly extends HEPA life. Differential pressure monitoring accurately determines replacement timing.

faqItems.q: How is cleanroom classification determined?
faqItems.a: Classification is based on airborne particle count according to ISO 14644-1. For example, ISO 5 is commonly used for operating rooms, while ISO 7-8 is typical for pharmaceutical production areas.

faqItems.q: Can HEPA filters be added to an existing ventilation system?
faqItems.a: HEPA filter integration is possible depending on the existing system's capacity and pressure drop. However, AHU revision is generally recommended for multi-stage filtration.

faqItems.q: Do you provide validation services?
faqItems.a: Yes, NOVVES provides validation services for all hygienic ventilation projects, including particle count testing, DOP/PAO filter integrity testing and pressure mapping.

faqItems.q: What should be considered when selecting an industrial fan?
faqItems.a: Air volume, pressure loss, operating temperature, ambient conditions (dust, humidity, corrosive gases) and noise level are the key selection criteria. The NOVVES engineering team determines the most suitable fan type for each project.

faqItems.q: Which fans are used in high temperature environments?
faqItems.a: NAUTILUS series centrifugal fans are designed for continuous operation up to 120°C. For higher temperatures, special materials and cooling solutions are applied.

faqItems.q: Is an ATEX certified fan required?
faqItems.a: ATEX certified BEAR series fans are mandatory in areas with explosive atmosphere risk (chemical, petrochemical, paint, grain silo, etc.).

faqItems.q: Can additional ventilation be added to existing facilities?
faqItems.a: Yes, capacity increases or new exhaust lines can be added to existing duct and fan systems. The most suitable solution is determined after a site survey.

faqItems.q: Which ventilation system should be preferred for poultry houses?
faqItems.a: Depending on house size and climate, tunnel ventilation (negative pressure) or cross ventilation systems are used. In hot climates, evaporative cooling pad integration is essential.

faqItems.q: How is ammonia level controlled?
faqItems.a: Continuous monitoring with ammonia sensors adjusts fan speeds automatically. Minimum ventilation rates keep ammonia concentration below safe limits.

faqItems.q: How is ventilation managed in winter months?
faqItems.a: In winter, minimum ventilation mode maintains air quality while minimizing heat loss. Variable speed fans and automatic inlet control create a balanced system.

faqItems.q: How much energy does the system consume?
faqItems.a: EC motor fan systems consume 30-50% less energy compared to conventional AC motors. The automation system runs fans only when needed, preventing energy waste.

trafoEnerjiOdalari.whyImportantP1: Transformer stations and energy rooms can reach dangerous temperature levels due to heat generated by electrical equipment. Excessive heat causes transformer oil degradation, cable insulation loss and system failures.
trafoEnerjiOdalari.whyImportantP2: NOVVES ensures temperature control in transformer rooms, medium voltage switchgear, generator rooms and UPS spaces with ventilation solutions designed to comply with TEDAS technical specifications.

faqItems.q: Why is ventilation mandatory in transformer rooms?
faqItems.a: Heat loss from transformers accumulates in the room, and ambient temperatures above 40°C shorten transformer life, causing oil degradation and insulation failures. TEDAS specifications mandate adequate ventilation.

faqItems.q: Which fan type is suitable for transformer rooms?
faqItems.a: Depending on room size and heat load, wall-type (OWL), roof-type (HERON) or duct-type (KOI) fans are used. Typically, an exhaust + fresh air supply combination is applied.

faqItems.q: How does thermostat-controlled operation work?
faqItems.a: When room temperature reaches the set upper limit, fans activate automatically and shut off when the temperature drops. Multiple stages provide energy savings.

faqItems.q: Are there special requirements for UPS rooms?
faqItems.a: Yes, hydrogen gas can form in battery groups during charging. Therefore, UPS rooms require appropriate ventilation and gas detection systems considering explosion risk.

trafoEnerjiOdalari.finalCtaTitle: Looking for a transformer room ventilation solution?
trafoEnerjiOdalari.finalCtaDesc: Contact our engineering team for heat load calculation, system design and quotation.

seraTarimsal.whyImportantP2: NOVVES provides evaporative cooling, natural/mechanical ventilation and smart climate control solutions for greenhouses and agricultural facilities. Each project is specifically designed based on regional climate data and crop requirements.

faqItems.q: What is the most suitable ventilation system for greenhouses?
faqItems.a: Depending on greenhouse size, location and crop type, negative pressure (exhaust + cooling pad) or positive pressure (overhead) systems are used. In hot climates, evaporative cooling integration is of great importance.

faqItems.q: How much temperature reduction can evaporative cooling achieve?
faqItems.a: A temperature reduction of 8-12°C can be achieved depending on the region's humidity level. Efficiency is higher in low-humidity regions.

faqItems.q: Is greenhouse ventilation necessary in winter?
faqItems.a: Yes, even in winter, minimum ventilation is needed for humidity control and CO2 supplementation. Otherwise, fungal diseases and plant stress may increase.

faqItems.q: Is remote monitoring and control possible?
faqItems.a: Yes, NOVVES smart climate control panels provide remote temperature, humidity and CO2 monitoring, fan control and alarm management through IoT infrastructure.

atexPatlamaKoruma.ctaPrimary: Get a Quote for Your Project
atexPatlamaKoruma.whyImportantTitle: Safety is Priority in Explosive Environments
atexPatlamaKoruma.whyImportantP1: In facilities such as chemical, petrochemical, paint, pharmaceutical, grain silos and mining, flammable gas, vapor and dust concentrations can create explosive atmospheres. Using standard equipment in these environments poses serious risks.
atexPatlamaKoruma.whyImportantP2: NOVVES BEAR series ATEX certified fans are designed according to explosive atmosphere classification (Zone 1, Zone 2, Zone 21, Zone 22). Spark-free materials, explosion-proof motors and special sealing solutions ensure safe ventilation.

advantages.desc: Appropriate fan selection and design according to gas group and temperature class.

advantages.title: End-to-End Solution

atexPatlamaKoruma.areasDesc: ATEX solutions for all industrial facilities with explosive atmosphere risks.

faqItems.q: What does ATEX certification mean?
faqItems.a: ATEX (ATmospheres EXplosibles) is the 2014/34/EU European Union directive that defines the design and manufacturing standards for equipment used in explosive environments. BEAR series fans are manufactured in compliance with this directive.

faqItems.q: What is the difference between Zone 1 and Zone 2?
faqItems.a: Zone 1 defines areas where explosive atmospheres may occasionally occur during normal operating conditions; Zone 2 defines areas where explosive atmospheres may only occur under abnormal conditions and for short periods.

faqItems.q: Which gas groups do you manufacture fans for?
faqItems.a: The BEAR series is manufactured in models suitable for gas groups IIA (propane, methane), IIB (ethylene) and IIC (hydrogen, acetylene).

faqItems.q: Can an ATEX fan be integrated into an existing system?
faqItems.a: Yes, ATEX certified fan integration into existing ventilation systems is possible. However, duct, connection and automation components must also be checked for ATEX compliance.

akilliOtomasyon.whyImportantP1: In modern buildings and industrial facilities, manual management of ventilation systems leads to inefficiency, energy waste and maintenance issues. Smart automation optimizes systems based on sensor data.
akilliOtomasyon.whyImportantP2: NOVVES automation solutions provide full control of ventilation systems with PLC-based MCC panels, variable frequency drive integration, BMS connectivity and IoT-based remote monitoring. Scenario-based operating modes minimize energy consumption.

faqItems.q: Can automation be added to an existing system?
faqItems.a: Yes, PLC panels, variable frequency drives and sensor integration can be added to existing fan and ventilation systems to upgrade automation levels.

faqItems.q: Which BMS protocols do you support?
faqItems.a: We support Modbus RTU/TCP, BACnet IP/MSTP and LON protocols. The most suitable integration method is determined based on your existing BMS infrastructure.

faqItems.q: Is internet connection required for remote monitoring?
faqItems.a: Yes, internet connectivity is required at the facility for IoT-based remote monitoring. Data transfer to the cloud platform is provided via 4G/5G modem or ethernet connection.

faqItems.q: How much energy does the automation system save?
faqItems.a: Variable speed fan control based on sensor data can achieve 30-50% energy savings. Night mode and vacant area scenarios provide additional savings.

konutHavalandirma.subtitle: Fresh air supply, humidity control and comfortable living space ventilation solutions for apartments, villas, residences and housing projects.
konutHavalandirma.ctaPrimary: Get a Quote for Your Project
konutHavalandirma.whyImportantP1: In modern residences, as insulation levels increase, natural air exchange has decreased, making indoor air quality a serious concern. Insufficient ventilation causes humidity, mold, odor and stale air buildup.
konutHavalandirma.whyImportantP2: NOVVES residential ventilation solutions create healthy, clean and comfortable living spaces with heat recovery fresh air supply that minimizes energy loss. Quiet operation and compact design ensure compatibility with all housing types.

faqItems.q: Does residential ventilation create energy loss?
faqItems.a: In systems with heat recovery units, up to 90% of the energy in exhaust air is recovered. This means fresh air supply is achieved with near-zero energy loss.

faqItems.q: How much noise does a bathroom fan make?
faqItems.a: SEAHORSE series bathroom fans operate very quietly at noise levels below 25 dB(A). The night mode option further reduces noise levels.

faqItems.q: Can a system be installed in existing homes?
faqItems.a: Yes, individual fresh air units that don't require ducts or compact ductwork can be used to install ventilation systems in existing homes.

faqItems.q: How often should filters be changed?
faqItems.a: Filter replacement is recommended every 3-6 months depending on usage intensity and outdoor air quality. Models with filter blockage indicators are available.

marinOffshore.ctaPrimary: Get a Quote for Your Project
marinOffshore.whyImportantP1: The marine environment presents challenges very different from land applications with saltwater, high humidity and aggressive atmospheric conditions. Standard ventilation equipment quickly corrodes and fails under these conditions.
marinOffshore.whyImportantP2: NOVVES marine ventilation solutions are designed with stainless steel (SS316), special coatings and marine classification society (DNV-GL, Lloyd's, BV) approvals. Reliable ventilation is provided in engine rooms, passenger spaces, cargo areas and special compartments.

faqItems.q: What classification approvals are available for marine fans?
faqItems.a: NOVVES marine fans can be manufactured with approvals from international marine classification societies including DNV-GL, Lloyd's Register, Bureau Veritas and ABS.

faqItems.q: Why is SS316 material preferred?
faqItems.a: SS316 stainless steel provides much longer-lasting performance in saltwater environments compared to standard steels due to its high corrosion resistance. It is especially preferred for above-deck and weatherproof areas.

faqItems.q: Which fans are used for engine room ventilation?
faqItems.a: High-volume radial or axial fans suitable for high temperature and exhaust gas conditions are used in engine rooms. BEAR series is preferred when exproof requirements apply.

faqItems.q: Can retrofit be done on existing vessels?
faqItems.a: Yes, retrofit solutions are available for ventilation system revision or capacity upgrades on existing vessels.

faqItems.q: Is there a minimum order quantity for custom manufacturing?
faqItems.a: No, since NOVVES works on a project basis, single-unit custom manufacturing orders are also accepted.

faqItems.q: What is the custom manufacturing lead time?
faqItems.a: Depending on product complexity, a delivery time of 4-12 weeks after design approval is applied. An accelerated production program is available for urgent projects.

faqItems.q: Is it possible to modify an existing fan?
faqItems.a: Yes, modification services such as impeller replacement, casing revision, motor upgrade or material change for existing fans are available.

faqItems.q: What testing and certification services are offered?
faqItems.a: Aerodynamic performance testing (flow, pressure, power), vibration analysis, noise measurement and project-specific test reports are prepared. Testing capabilities compliant with AMCA, ISO and EN standards are available.

cfdDanismanlik.subtitle: Simulation-supported engineering services with Computational Fluid Dynamics (CFD) for ventilation, smoke extraction and heat transfer projects.
cfdDanismanlik.ctaPrimary: Get a Quote for Your Project
cfdDanismanlik.whyImportantP1: Knowing the real performance of ventilation and smoke extraction systems before installation prevents costly mistakes and enables documentation of regulatory compliance. CFD analysis visualizes airflow, temperature distribution and smoke spread through 3D computer simulation.
cfdDanismanlik.whyImportantP2: The NOVVES CFD team performs project-specific simulations using ANSYS Fluent and OpenFOAM platforms. Engineering support is provided for jet fan placement, smoke extraction time, comfort analysis, heat load calculation and system optimization.

steps.label: Project Definition
steps.desc: Geometry, boundary conditions, fire scenario or ventilation requirements definition

steps.label: 3D Modeling & Mesh
steps.desc: CAD geometry creation or import, computational mesh generation

steps.label: Simulation & Solution
steps.desc: Flow, temperature and smoke solution with RANS/LES turbulence models

steps.label: Reporting & Optimization
steps.desc: Result visualization, performance evaluation, improvement recommendations

systemComponents.desc: Smoke spread, visibility distance and evacuation time analysis in fire scenarios.

systemComponents.desc: Determining jet fan count, position and direction angle through simulation in car parks and tunnels.

systemComponents.desc: Temperature, air velocity and PMV/PPD comfort evaluation in offices, shopping centers and assembly areas.

systemComponents.title: Heat Transfer Analysis
systemComponents.desc: Temperature distribution simulation in transformer rooms, data centers and industrial facilities.

systemComponents.title: Cleanroom Airflow
systemComponents.desc: Particle distribution, laminar flow verification and pressure cascade analysis in cleanrooms.

systemComponents.title: System Optimization
systemComponents.desc: CFD review of existing ventilation systems and performance improvement recommendations.

areas.description: Jet fan placement and CO/smoke extraction simulation

areas.title: Tunnel Ventilation Projects
areas.description: Fire scenario and air velocity analysis

areas.title: Data Center Cooling
areas.description: Rack-level temperature distribution and hotspot analysis

areas.title: Smoke Control in High-Rise Buildings
areas.description: Stairwell pressurization and smoke zoning simulation

faqItems.q: How long does a CFD analysis take?
faqItems.a: Depending on project complexity, it takes 1-4 weeks. Simple single-scenario analyses are shorter, while multi-fire scenarios or optimization studies take longer.

faqItems.q: What information is needed for CFD analysis?
faqItems.a: Architectural plans (DWG/Revit), fan/grille placement information, operating conditions (temperature, flow rate, etc.) and fire scenario definitions if applicable are required.

faqItems.q: Is a CFD report accepted as an official document?
faqItems.a: The CFD report is used as a supporting engineering document in municipality and fire department approval processes. Signed and sealed reports are provided for each project.

faqItems.q: Can I only get the CFD analysis service?
faqItems.a: Yes, NOVVES also offers independent CFD consultancy services. Simulation and reporting services can be obtained without being tied to fan and equipment sales.

cfdDanismanlik.finalCtaTitle: Do you need a CFD analysis?
cfdDanismanlik.finalCtaDesc: Contact our CFD engineering team for project definition, simulation and quotation.
